methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a Seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Graphyte at $200M Series A, Fourth Power at $150M Series A, and Steady Energy at $110M Series A), triangulated against a broader landscape of nine private frontier-tech peers and one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $11M total raised as a proxy base and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables yields a $150M median valuation, with a range of $110M–$200M. The median peer valuation reflects Series A positioning; adjusting for Furno's current Seed stage introduces material downside relative to that benchmark.

Triangulating both methods produces a low estimate of $23M, a median of $89M, and a high of $172M. The wide range reflects the inherent tension between bottom-up (stage-progression) and top-down (peer) approaches at early stage. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-peer sample, which skew later-stage, and the applicability of Series A comparables to a Seed-stage company absent clear revenue or unit economics data. A valuation in the $40M–$110M range appears defensible; validation would require clarity on technical differentiation and path-to-Series A metrics.
